extern type Number;
extern type Bool;
cap Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_sub(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
extern fn js_is_zero(n: Number): Bool;
impl Number: Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_sub(a, b)) }

fn count_down(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 0 } else { count_down(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn main(): Number = count_down(5)

===EXPECT===
# Self-recursion: `count_down` references itself inside its body. v1 handles
# this optimistically — the fn is classified dep-free (its only cap Perform
# has a default impl), cloned as `count_down__lto`, and the `Sub.sub` call is
# inlined to a direct `js_sub(a, b)` call.
#
# v1 subtlety (noted, not a bug): the recursive call in the cloned body is
# rewritten to call the ORIGINAL `count_down` (CPS form) rather than
# `count_down__lto`. The plan anticipates "self-recursion replaces recursive
# calls with the clone"; v1 doesn't do that rewrite yet, so the tail recursion
# passes through the cap-bundle-threading CPS form. Functionally correct;
# perf-leaky. Assertions pin down what v1 emits today.
#
# Key invariants:
#   - `count_down__lto` exists with `js_sub(a, b)` directly.
#   - `__main_cps` calls `count_down__lto(...)` (not the CPS `count_down`).
#   - original CPS `count_down` also exists (needed for the recursive call
#     from inside the clone, per v1 limitation above).
count_down__lto
js_sub(a, b)
function count_down(__caps, n, __k)
__main_cps
(__caps, 5, __k)
return count_down(__caps,
!count_down__lto(__caps, ((a) =>
